content:
# Deuteronomy Chapter 18 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), Folios 109v–109r, LC PDF pages 215–216 | **Source Images:** DEU_018_page_001.jpg, DEU_018_page_002.jpg | **LXX:** Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), pa_0154–pa_0155 — DEU_018_vaticanus_page_001.jpg, DEU_018_vaticanus_page_002.jpg

---

**1** There shall not be for the Levitical priests—all the tribe of Levi—a portion and inheritance with Yisra'el; the fire offerings of the LORD and his inheritance they shall eat.

**2** And he shall have no inheritance in the midst of his brothers; the LORD is his inheritance, as he spoke to him.

**3** And this shall be the priests' due [mishpat ha-kohanim] from the people, from those who offer a sacrifice—whether ox or sheep: he shall give to the priest the shoulder and the two cheeks and the stomach.

**4** The first of your grain, your new wine, and your fresh oil, and the first of the fleece of your flock, you shall give to him.

**5** For him the LORD your God has chosen from all your tribes to stand to minister in the name of the LORD, him and his sons, forever.

**6** And if a Levite comes from one of your gates, from all Yisra'el, where he sojourns, and comes with all the desire of his being to the place which the LORD will choose—

**7** then he shall minister in the name of the LORD his God, like all his brothers the Levites who stand there before the LORD.

**8** Portion equal to portion they shall eat, besides his sales from [the estate of] the fathers.

**9** When you come to the land which the LORD your God is giving you, you shall not learn to do according to the abominations of those nations.

**10** There shall not be found among you one who makes his son or his daughter pass through the fire [ma'avir beno u-vito ba-esh], a diviner of divinations [qosem qesamim], a soothsayer [me'onen], or an augur [menachesh], or a sorcerer [mekhashef],

**11** or a charmer [chover chaver], or one who inquires of a familiar spirit [sho'el ov], or a wizard [yidde'oni], or one who inquires of the dead [doresh el ha-metim].

**12** For everyone who does these things is an abomination to the LORD, and on account of these abominations the LORD your God is dispossessing them before you.

**13** You shall be wholehearted [tamim] with the LORD your God.

**14** For these nations which you are dispossessing listen to soothsayers and to diviners, but you—the LORD your God has not given [this] to you.

**15** A prophet [navi] from your midst, from your brothers, like me, the LORD your God will raise up for you—to him you shall listen.

**16** According to all that you asked of the LORD your God at Horev on the day of the assembly, saying, "Let me not again hear the voice of the LORD my God and let me not again see this great fire, that I may not die."

**17** And the LORD said to me, "They have done well in what they have spoken.

**18** A prophet I will raise up for them from the midst of their brothers, like you; and I will put my words in his mouth, and he shall speak to them all that I command him.

**19** And it shall be that the man who does not listen to my words which he speaks in my name—I myself will require [it] of him.

**20** But the prophet who presumes to speak a word in my name which I have not commanded him to speak, or who speaks in the name of other gods—that prophet shall die.

**21** And if you say in your heart, 'How shall we know the word which the LORD has not spoken?'—

**22** that which the prophet speaks in the name of the LORD, and the thing does not come about and does not come, that is the thing which the LORD has not spoken; in presumptuousness the prophet spoke it; you shall not be afraid of him.

---

## EMET's Textual Notes

**v.1 — chelqo ve-nachalato:** "His portion and inheritance." Two synonymous terms: cheleq (portion/share) and nachalah (inheritance/patrimony). The Levites receive no land inheritance — their inheritance is the LORD himself (v.2: YHWH hu nachalato). This double phrase appears identically in the MT/LC.

**v.2 — YHWH hu nachalato:** "The LORD is his inheritance." A profound theological declaration — the Levite's portion is not agricultural land but the divine presence and service itself. LXX (p. 154) κύριος αὐτοῦ κληρονομία — "the LORD [is] his inheritance" — exact.

**v.3 — mishpat ha-kohanim:** "The priests' due." Mishpat here carries the sense of legal entitlement/due. The three portions — the shoulder (zeroa'), the two cheeks (le-chayayim), and the stomach (ve-ha-qevah) — are the priest's fixed perquisite from each slaughtered ox or sheep.

**v.4 — reshit deganekha:** "The first of your grain." The list: grain (dagan), new wine (tirosh), fresh oil (yitshar), and the first fleece (reshit gez). These four items represent the agricultural cycle's first-fruits due to the Levitical priesthood.

**v.6 — be-khol-avvat nafsho:** "With all the desire of his being." Avvah (אַוָּה) = desire/longing. The Levite may present himself at the central sanctuary motivated by genuine spiritual longing — his service is then valid.

**v.8 — chelqo chelqam yokhelu:** "Portion equal to portion they shall eat." Literally "his portion like their portions they shall eat." The Levite who comes from the country has equal share with the resident Levites at the central sanctuary. LXX (p. 154) μερίδα μερίδος φάγεται — exact cognate structure.

**v.10 — ma'avir beno u-vito ba-esh:** "One who makes his son or his daughter pass through the fire." The first and most severe item in the divination list — child sacrifice (or dedicatory fire ritual) associated with the Molech cult. LXX (p. 154) ὃς διάγει τὸν υἱὸν αὐτοῦ ἢ τὴν θυγατέρα αὐτοῦ ἐν πυρί.

**v.10 — qosem qesamim:** "A diviner of divinations." Cognate construction intensifying the noun. Qosem (קוֹסֵם) is the general term for a diviner. Six forbidden practices follow in vv.10-11:
1. qosem qesamim — diviner of divinations
2. me'onen — soothsayer (from 'anan, cloud-reading or enchantment)
3. menachesh — augur (from nachash, omen-reading)
4. mekhashef — sorcerer
5. chover chaver — charmer (literally "one who binds a binding")
6. sho'el ov — inquirer of a ghost/familiar spirit (ov = ghost-spirit)
7. yidde'oni — wizard/knowing-spirit (from yada', to know)
8. doresh el ha-metim — one who inquires of the dead (necromancer)

LXX (p. 154) has equivalent Greek terms: μαντευόμενος, κληδονιζόμενος, οἰωνιζόμενος, φαρμακός, ἐπαοιδός, ἐγγαστρίμυθος, τερατοσκόπος, ἐπερωτῶν τοὺς νεκρούς.

**v.13 — tamim tihyeh im-YHWH Elohekha:** "You shall be wholehearted with the LORD your God." Tamim (תָּמִים) = whole/complete/unblemished. The same root used for sacrificial animal standards. The positive command following the list of prohibitions: the alternative to divination is wholeheartedness toward God alone. LXX τέλειος ἔσῃ ἐναντίον κυρίου — "perfect/complete you shall be before the LORD."

**v.15 — navi ka-mokha:** "A prophet like me." The prophet-like-Moses oracle. The singular navi here — unlike the regular flow of prophets anticipated in v.22 — has been read by Jewish and Christian tradition as pointing to a paradigmatic figure. The LC reads clearly: navi mi-qirbekha me-achekha kamoni yaqim lekha YHWH Elohekha, "a prophet from your midst, from your brothers, like me, the LORD your God will raise up for you."

**v.15 — elav tishma'un:** "To him you shall listen." The identical verb (shama'/listen) that anchors the Shema (Deut 6:4). The call to listen to the prophet-like-Moses echoes the foundational command of Israel's relationship with the LORD.

**v.18 — ve-natati devaray be-fiv:** "And I will put my words in his mouth." The authoritative criterion of true prophecy: the LORD's words, not the prophet's own. Compare Jer 1:9 — the same image. LXX (p. 155) καὶ δώσω τὸ ῥῆμά μου ἐν τῷ στόματι αὐτοῦ.

**v.19 — anokhi edrosh me'immo:** "I myself will require [it] of him." The LORD takes personal responsibility for accountability toward the true prophet — anyone who refuses to hear him will be held to account by God directly.

**v.20 — asher lo-tsivitiv ledabber:** "Which I have not commanded him to speak." Two categories of false prophecy: (1) speaking in the LORD's name without a divine commission; (2) speaking in the name of other gods. Both result in death.

**v.22 — lo yagur mimmennu:** "You shall not be afraid of him." The false prophet is stripped of the fear his pretense generates. The criterion of fulfilled prophecy (ha-davar lo yihyeh ve-lo yavo) is practical and public. LXX (p. 155) οὐκ εὐλαβηθήσεσθε ἀπ᾽ αὐτοῦ — exact.

**LXX comparison (Vaticanus pp. 154–155):** Vaticanus page 154 contains the opening of ch. 18 through the divination list, and page 155 continues through the false-prophet test (v.22). The LXX is closely aligned with the MT throughout. Notable: (1) v.13 — LXX τέλειος renders tamim as "perfect" (LXX preference for perfection language); (2) v.15 — the prophet-like-Moses passage shows no major variants between MT and LXX; (3) the divination list in vv.10-11 — the LXX Greek terms attempt to map the Hebrew categories onto Hellenistic categories of magical practice, with moderate success. The list order is preserved.

— *EMET*
